rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports and data types.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees.
generic_rules: Use explicit type declarations in AGREE equations to match the actual data types of AADL features.
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions unless they are explicitly exposed as typed variables.
Generate range assumptions using visible feature names and concrete bounds from the requirement.
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs without referencing implementation-only subcomponent paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Assumptions and guarantees must be syntactically valid within the AGREE annex scope.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the expected data types of the referenced features.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL timing properties like Deadline or Period directly in AGREE expressions.
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent ports in component type contracts.
Do not generate range assumptions for Boolean or opaque data types without numeric scalar representation.
irrelevant_topics: AADL model fusion and validation repair patterns
